D_phrase: HTTP delay at caller travel-plan produces no intrinsic slow-span on the hosting service and a loud secondary 90s GC pause appears on a downstream queue-fed service.
R_phrase: Agent selected the loudest infrastructure hotspot as RC without testing whether that hotspot was downstream of and back-pressured by the still-unexamined SLO origin.

D_phrase: TimeSkew on the target pod produces no latency, CPU, or error signature at any span; symptoms are purely application-logic-level.
R_phrase: Agent executed a default drill-to-slowest-leaf search assuming the SLO origin must be a leaf with an observable magnitude, rather than considering a symptom-silent injection target.

D_phrase: JVM-level MySQL latency injection is masked by a crowd of high-magnitude environmental noises (rabbitmq DNS, consign NonUniqueResultException, order-service DB tail) all firing simultaneously.
R_phrase: Agent oscillated across many independent hypotheses for tens of rounds and committed to a compromise multi-RC pair that averaged across noises rather than disambiguating which service hosted the JVM hook.

D_phrase: Request-side HTTP delay on travel2->seat leaves a literal connection-time signature exactly matching the injection magnitude on travel2, competing with a dramatic worker-node load spike on an unrelated pod.
R_phrase: Agent abandoned a numerically-matching fault-magnitude signal on the actual target in favor of a more evocative infrastructure-overload narrative on a bystander node.

D_phrase: Container-kill produces both a local pod-restart signal and a correlated-in-time MySQL connection-abort burst (the pod's own connections being torn down), offering two equally-visible explanations.
R_phrase: After observing the narrower and sufficient pod-restart cause, agent swapped to a broader-scope infrastructure cause because a correlated-in-time signal appeared, inverting the causal direction on the restart window.

D_phrase: Container-kill-like perturbation on food-service is masked by the permanent rabbitmq-DNS ambient noise plus a loud GC avalanche on order-service, while the target service itself has a subtle signature.
R_phrase: Agent oscillated between the two loudest noise signatures, committed to a compromise multi-RC pair, and treated the subtle food-service trace as ambient background rather than a target candidate.

D_phrase: NetworkChaos on an egress edge produces a caller-selective failure pattern (callee responds fine to other callers) but this asymmetry is only visible by comparing caller-distribution across trace subsets.
R_phrase: Agent observed the selective-failure asymmetry but did not use it to flip attribution from callees to the caller hosting the egress chaos rule; defaulted to blaming the callees.

D_phrase: PodChaos on ui-dashboard and food-service produces subtle signatures while a mid-stack CPU hotspot on seat-service is dramatic; rabbitmq-DNS noise masks food-service further.
R_phrase: Agent drilled to a visible mid-stack CPU hotspot instead of the subtle-signature injection targets, following the drill-to-leaf reflex where neither proper leaf had an observable anomaly.

D_phrase: Environment has permanent rabbitmq-DNS misconfiguration always emitting UnknownHostException errors, producing the loudest unambiguous unavailability signature available.
R_phrase: Agent interpreted the chronic baseline DNS noise as an active ongoing outage and assigned it as RC without baseline-vs-abnormal comparison.

D_phrase: Preserve-service injection amplifies the ambient rabbitmq-DNS signature while preserve's own signal is subtle.
R_phrase: Agent adopted the amplified chronic rabbitmq DNS noise as RC and relegated the injection target to downstream-victim status.

D_phrase: Response-body corruption at route-plan->travel2 leaves no latency/error signature on travel2's server spans; the only artifact is NPEs in route-plan's client code.
R_phrase: Agent correctly localized the fault to an HTTP edge but committed to the wrong endpoint (picked a higher-traffic sibling with no anomaly over the actual injected service).

D_phrase: DNSRandom fault produces sporadic connection resets and retries on the target's DB client but no single loud anomaly; DNS layer is not directly observable via the agent's tools.
R_phrase: Agent executed the drill-to-slowest-leaf reflex on any service with slow DB queries, producing an off-fault-class RC because no observable leaf actually belonged to the DNS fault.

D_phrase: HTTP response delay at travel-plan->train-service is visible only as slow outgoing calls from travel-plan; downstream services on the same trace also look slow by propagation.
R_phrase: Agent sampled one slow trace, picked whichever downstream leaf had the most-visible span in that trace, and committed without tracing the injected call edge specifically.

D_phrase: JVMMemoryStress at travel-plan makes the injected service slow in a way indistinguishable from 'its downstream is slow' from the trace perspective; a correlated worker-node load spike appears nearby.
R_phrase: Agent attributed the travel-plan slowness to the most infrastructure-anomalous downstream rather than to the JVM-stressed injection target, without probing the target's own JVM/heap metrics.

D_phrase: Container-kill on food-service is masked by the chronic rabbitmq-DNS noise which amplifies whenever food-service is perturbed in any way.
R_phrase: Agent preferred the louder unambiguous chronic DNS signature over the subtle pod-restart signal on the actual target.

D_phrase: Response-body corruption on food-service->travel-service leaves no localized anomaly, only decodable garbage at various points.
R_phrase: Agent defaulted under uncertainty to the highest-traffic-looking service on the adjacent edge rather than acknowledging the uncertainty and disambiguating.

D_phrase: Container-kill on preserve-service produces a short restart window where preserve's own outgoing calls fail, visible in traces as A->B edge failures originating at A.
R_phrase: Agent read failed outgoing calls as evidence that callees were unavailable, without cross-checking pod-restart timing to attribute the source side of the failure.

D_phrase: HTTP request abort on route-plan->travel2 leaves route-plan as the side with the client-side anomaly; travel2 just sees fewer successful calls.
R_phrase: Agent could not distinguish which endpoint of A->B hosts the chaos rule and defaulted to a sibling service under uncertainty.

D_phrase: JVMReturn at station-service produces semantically-wrong but structurally-correct outputs; downstream route-service is the first place the error becomes visible.
R_phrase: Agent blamed the downstream service where the confusion first materialized, unable to distinguish 'X produces wrong output' from 'X's downstream is broken'.

D_phrase: JVMException at travel-plan's controller produces 500 responses that propagate as client-visible errors to services travel-plan calls downstream.
R_phrase: Agent traced the 500 backwards to whichever downstream was called in the same failing trace, picking the downstream-called service as culprit rather than the exception-hosting target.

D_phrase: Request-side HTTP delay at travel-plan->seat leaves the actual caller travel-plan with slow outgoing spans indistinguishable from 'seat is slow downstream'.
R_phrase: Agent drilled to the slowest DB-leaf downstream, ignoring that the injection could live at the caller's outgoing request path.

D_phrase: JVMException at order-other's refresh endpoint produces a literal RuntimeException signature in logs; a dramatic but coincidental memory-growth pattern on seat-service competes for attention.
R_phrase: Agent saw the directly-matching exception evidence on the target but discounted it in favor of a more elaborate multi-turn infrastructure narrative on the memory-climbing neighbor.

D_phrase: Bandwidth limit on preserve->travel edge makes calls hang, and the hang propagates one more level into travel's internal calls (to basic) which also appear slow by propagation.
R_phrase: Agent drilled one layer past the actual bandwidth-limited edge to an internal downstream call whose slowness was pure propagation, not source.

D_phrase: Clean HTTP delay on preserve->seat is drowned by preserve's retry cascade amplifying the ambient rabbitmq-DNS noise.
R_phrase: Agent picked the chronically-noisy unavailable-looking rabbitmq over the clean but subtle delay signature on the actual injected edge.

D_phrase: NetworkCorrupt on route-plan->travel-plan produces garbled responses; downstream siblings of travel-plan (like seat) inherit elevated latency through propagation.
R_phrase: Agent reached for a sibling of B based on raw anomaly magnitude rather than applying an edge-side attribution protocol.

D_phrase: HTTP response delay on route-plan->travel leaves route-service (a co-downstream of route-plan) as a plausible but causally-unrelated slow-looking candidate.
R_phrase: Agent drilled to a sibling on the same fan-out rather than distinguishing which of the two route-plan callees hosted the delay.

D_phrase: Bandwidth limit on seat->config cascades through seat's callers (travel, travel2, route-plan) making them all look slow.
R_phrase: Agent defaulted to whichever sibling-of-A looked most infrastructurally-anomalous rather than committing to the seat->config edge endpoints.

D_phrase: NetworkDelay on seat->travel-plan edge produces diffuse latency spread across multiple consumers; no single service is obviously the source.
R_phrase: Agent committed to a compromise pair of visibly-latent but unrelated services rather than identifying either endpoint of the injected edge.

D_phrase: Bandwidth on station->basic edge makes basic appear slow to many callers; travel-service is one such observer.
R_phrase: Agent picked a non-A non-B service that merely observes the effect, defaulting to downstream-observer blame under edge-attribution uncertainty.

D_phrase: NetworkLoss on travel-plan->ui-dashboard egress edge has no precedent in agent's fault model; peers of travel-plan show ordinary latency variance.
R_phrase: Agent picked a peer of the injected caller because it has no concept of egress-to-dashboard chaos and defaulted to peer-sibling blame.

D_phrase: Response-body replacement on travel->basic leaves noisy signatures across many neighboring services but no single dominant signal.
R_phrase: Agent exhausted budget oscillating, committed to a compromise pair that does not intersect either endpoint of the injected edge.

D_phrase: HTTP request abort on travel2->seat cascades through basic-service (a downstream of seat) which also appears slow.
R_phrase: Agent defaulted to blaming a third service entirely, selecting based on anomaly magnitude rather than edge-endpoint membership.

D_phrase: Response-body replacement on travel2->basic corrupts data visible at seat-service (which uses basic), making seat look anomalous.
R_phrase: Agent picked a sibling/downstream with visible anomaly rather than one of the injected edge endpoints.

D_phrase: Response-code replacement on food->train-food is drowned by chronic rabbitmq-DNS noise plus a seat-service CPU hotspot elsewhere on the call graph.
R_phrase: Agent let the chronic noise mask the injected edge and defaulted to drilling to the visible downstream CPU hotspot instead.

D_phrase: JVMException at preserve cascades into food/notification/delivery retries, amplifying the chronic rabbitmq DNS noise into the loudest visible pattern.
R_phrase: Agent followed the amplified chronic-noise trail to rabbitmq rather than tracing the upstream preserve perturbation back to its injection source.

D_phrase: NetworkPartition on preserve->seat triggers the same rabbitmq-noise amplification cascade via preserve's downstream dependencies.
R_phrase: Agent again adopted the amplified chronic DNS noise as RC rather than isolating the preserve->seat network-level fault.

D_phrase: HTTP response delay on preserve->security is one of several latency candidates; preserve->price is also a loud path in the same trace.
R_phrase: Agent drilled to a non-adjacent hotspot without specifically tracing preserve's outgoing security call, failing to apply the injected-edge constraint.

D_phrase: Bandwidth limit on security->mysql makes MySQL-adjacent metrics (container.cpu.time) plateau and appear frozen because it is starved of requests, not paused.
R_phrase: Agent inferred PROCESS_PAUSED from the frozen-metric pattern without checking whether the silence was upstream-starvation or actual process pause, and missed the co-RC security-service.

D_phrase: NetworkDelay on travel-plan->seat injection produces diffuse latency with no dominant signature visible at any single edge.
R_phrase: Agent extended the drill-to-leaf exploration for 58 rounds and still committed to a downstream DB-query hotspot rather than the injected edge.

D_phrase: Response-code replacement on security->order leaves order-other-service (a near-twin of order-service) with ambiguous signals; both are DB-backed and both see some disruption.
R_phrase: Agent picked the near-twin sibling without disambiguating the two near-identical services on the injected edge.

D_phrase: HTTP response abort on travel->basic is subtle, while ts-admin-user-service has a 107x CPU spike completely off the SLO call path; shared-node narrative allows bridging the two.
R_phrase: Agent picked an off-call-graph service with the loudest CPU spike and wove an infrastructure-contention narrative to connect it to the SLO without causal validation against the call graph.

D_phrase: HTTP response delay on travel->basic makes basic's callees appear slow by propagation; seat-service is one such co-downstream candidate.
R_phrase: Agent picked a sibling of the callee on the same call region rather than committing to the travel->basic injected endpoints.

D_phrase: HTTP response delay on route-plan->travel2 leaves travel-service (near-twin of travel2 and co-downstream of route-plan) as a structurally-symmetric alternative.
R_phrase: Agent swapped in the near-twin sibling for the actual callee, unable to disambiguate travel vs travel2 on the route-plan fan-out.

D_phrase: JVMLatency at config-service makes config intrinsically slow; its callers (including basic-service) also appear slow on their config calls.
R_phrase: Agent attributed the slowness to a caller rather than to the JVM-stressed service itself, reversing the intrinsic-slowness attribution.

D_phrase: NetworkPartition on basic->travel produces cascading failures through travel's downstream siblings like seat-service.
R_phrase: Agent blamed the sibling with visible anomaly rather than the injected edge endpoints, applying the standard edge-direction default.

D_phrase: Bandwidth limit on basic->preserve (possibly reverse-labeled) produces diffuse signatures; seat-service has the most CPU-anomalous pattern.
R_phrase: Under exhaustion, agent defaulted to whichever service looked most CPU-anomalous rather than committing to the injected edge.

D_phrase: Response-code replacement on basic->route makes basic's other downstream (price-service) idle because basic stops calling it; price's metrics appear frozen.
R_phrase: Agent inferred PROCESS_PAUSED from a quiet service without checking whether the silence was upstream-starvation or actual pause, and missed the basic->route injected edge entirely.

D_phrase: DEFERRED: dataset labeling mismatch; DB meta says ts-config-service, datapack name says ts-food-service-container-kill. Not a genuine agent error.
R_phrase: DEFERRED: dataset_anomaly, not classifiable as a reasoning defect.

D_phrase: Bandwidth limit on route-plan->travel makes travel2 (near-twin of travel, co-downstream of route-plan) appear in the symmetric position.
R_phrase: Agent swapped travel for travel2, unable to distinguish the two near-twins on the injected edge.

D_phrase: Response-body replacement on seat->order-other amplifies rabbitmq noise through order-other's retry cascade; order-service is a near-twin of order-other.
R_phrase: Agent combined two independent defects into a compromise dual-RC answer: chronic-noise adoption plus near-twin confusion.

D_phrase: Container-kill on travel-plan produces a restart window where its outgoing calls to basic-service fail.
R_phrase: Agent again misread the caller's failed outgoing calls during the restart window as callee unavailability, failing to cross-check pod-restart timing.
